text: BetFrnd's TEMPLATE.md flagged scripts/rename.ts as missing KNOWN_FILES + \bMLabs\b substitution (recommendations #13, #14, #17) but the fix was never implemented in BetFrnd itself. When porting BetFrnd's recommendations back to the MLabs template, always grep BetFrnd's actual code — recommended fixes in TEMPLATE.md aren't a guarantee the fix exists; sometimes lessons stay documented and unimplemented for sprints. Audited 2026-05-23 during template-hardening plan.

When porting learnings from one fork back to a template, the lesson prose often describes a symptom (e.g. 'BetterAuth cookies don't work over HTTP') without naming the load-bearing code line that needs to change. The /mlabs-review pass MUST grep the cited files in the source fork's actual code, not just trust the lesson's narrative. Example from 2026-05-23 template-hardening review: TEMPLATE.md #20 + #28 and learnings 20/64/65/121 all described BetterAuth cookie-domain issues but never pointed at apps/web/src/lib/auth/index.ts:40 where the env.BETTER_AUTH_URL ?? https://$REPLIT_DEV_DOMAIN fallback actually lives. Without reading that file, the original plan missed adding a task for it.

Don't pin Stripe API version in template's stripe-client.ts. The Stripe Node SDK pins its target wire version internally and Stripe maintains backward-compat within a wire version. BetFrnd's shipped stripe-client.ts deliberately omits apiVersion with a comment explaining this; following that precedent. Reversed an in-progress 'pin at /mlabs-code time' decision on 2026-05-23 after reading BetFrnd's actual code. Generalizes: when locking template decisions about 3rd-party SDK config, verify against the SDK's own guidance + the source fork's shipped code, not abstract first-principles reasoning.
